User Story: As a user I can sign in with Google so I can access the portal
Acceptance Criteria: User clicks Google button → Mujarrad OAuth → JWT stored → redirected to /home

User Story: As a user I am redirected to login if I try to access a protected page
Acceptance Criteria: Unauthenticated user hitting /home gets redirected to /login

User Story: As a user I can log out and my session is cleared
Acceptance Criteria: JWT removed from storage. User redirected to /login

User Story: As a user I can sign in with email and password with 2FA
Acceptance Criteria: Email/password registration + TOTP 2FA

User Story: As a corporate user I can sign in with Microsoft SSO
Acceptance Criteria: Microsoft OAuth flow integrated

User Story: As a new user I complete a multi-step onboarding form tailored to my user type
Acceptance Criteria: 4 onboarding paths (investor/company/gov/startup). 2-4 fields per step. Progress bar. Data saved to Mujarrad

User Story: As a user I see my profile completeness score with prompts to fill missing sections
Acceptance Criteria: Score shown on Partner Home. Clicking prompt navigates to incomplete section

User Story: As a user I can upload documents (company registration / mandates / MOUs / credentials)
Acceptance Criteria: File upload. Files stored and linked to partner profile in Mujarrad

User Story: As a user I can edit my profile and changes are version-tracked
Acceptance Criteria: Edit form pre-filled with current data. Mujarrad versioning logs changes

User Story: As a user I can optionally enable a public-facing partner card with privacy controls
Acceptance Criteria: Toggle public/private. Choose which fields visible. Preview card

User Story: As an admin I can review partner documents and approve/reject verification badges
Acceptance Criteria: Verification queue. Approve/reject with reason. Badge shown on partner card

User Story: As a user I can create an organization during onboarding
Acceptance Criteria: Org created with name + business profile. User becomes owner

User Story: As an org owner I can invite members by email
Acceptance Criteria: Enter email → invitee receives link → signs up/logs in with Gmail → joins org

User Story: As an invited user I can join an org by signing up or logging in with Gmail
Acceptance Criteria: Invitation link → Google OAuth → auto-added to org. Full access

User Story: As an org member I see the same dashboard / engagements / documents as all other members
Acceptance Criteria: No role-based restrictions in MVP. Equal access for all members

User Story: As an org owner I can manage team members in settings
Acceptance Criteria: View members list. Remove member option

User Story: As an admin I can assign role-based access within an org
Acceptance Criteria: Owner/Admin/Member roles with different permission levels

User Story: As a user I see my Partner Home dashboard after login
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard shows: welcome + profile summary + action items + relationships + activity feed

User Story: As a user I see Action Required items (signatures needed / info requested / new matches)
Acceptance Criteria: Action cards with direct links to resolve each item

User Story: As a user I see my active relationships with current status and tier progress
Acceptance Criteria: Relationship cards showing partner name + status + tier + progress bar

User Story: As a user I see a recent activity feed
Acceptance Criteria: Chronological list of events: stage changes / docs uploaded / signatures / matches

User Story: As a user I see my portfolio summary on the dashboard
Acceptance Criteria: Asset count + total value + sector breakdown

User Story: As an admin I can manually create a match between two partners
Acceptance Criteria: Select two partners → create match → both see match card

User Story: As a user I see match suggestions with match cards (who / why / strength score)
Acceptance Criteria: Match list page. Each card shows partner info + match reason + score

User Story: As a user I can accept or decline a match suggestion
Acceptance Criteria: Accept/decline buttons. Two-way: both must accept before connection

User Story: As a user I can filter matches by country / sector / investment size / entity type
Acceptance Criteria: Filter controls on matches page. Results update in real-time

User Story: As a user I see my full match history (shown / accepted / rejected / pending)
Acceptance Criteria: History tab on matches page with status per match

User Story: As a user I see an opportunity board with active opportunities ranked by relevance
Acceptance Criteria: Wider Labs powered feed of opportunities

User Story: As a user I can see an AI explanation of why I was matched with a partner
Acceptance Criteria: Wider Labs natural language match explanation

User Story: Wider Labs auto-generates match suggestions within 24h of profile completion
Acceptance Criteria: Automated matching algorithm replaces manual admin matching

User Story: As a user I see a relationship dashboard grouped by status
Acceptance Criteria: List view: new / in conversation / MOU stage / active / dormant

User Story: As a user I see a chronological timeline for each relationship
Acceptance Criteria: Timeline of: status changes / documents / meetings / signatures

User Story: As a user both parties must confirm status progression (introduced → engaged → negotiating → formalised → active)
Acceptance Criteria: Status change requires both parties to confirm. Notification sent

User Story: As a user I can log a meeting with date / attendees / summary / outcome
Acceptance Criteria: Meeting form. Entry appended to relationship timeline

User Story: As a user I can share documents privately within a relationship
Acceptance Criteria: Upload doc → visible only to both parties. Stored per relationship

User Story: As a user I can add internal notes and tags to a relationship
Acceptance Criteria: Notes visible only to me. Tags for quick filtering

User Story: As a user I receive alerts when a relationship is inactive or the other party updates their profile
Acceptance Criteria: In-app notification. Configurable thresholds

User Story: Wider Labs calculates relationship health scores dynamically
Acceptance Criteria: Score based on recency / activity / stage / engagement depth

User Story: As an admin I see a Kanban view of all relationships
Acceptance Criteria: Drag-and-drop Kanban columns by status

User Story: As a user I can upload and download documents in my org vault
Acceptance Criteria: Upload files. List view with name / type / date / size. Download

User Story: As a user documents are tied to specific engagements / relationships / journey tiers
Acceptance Criteria: Documents linked to context (relationship / journey). Visible in timeline

User Story: As a user document changes are version-tracked
Acceptance Criteria: Upload new version. Previous versions accessible

User Story: As a user I can set per-document permissions
Acceptance Criteria: Control who can view/download each document

User Story: As a user I can draw or type my signature on a document
Acceptance Criteria: Canvas-based signature pad. Type option. Preview before submit

User Story: As a user (or SIA admin) I can request signatures from one or multiple org members
Acceptance Criteria: Select signers → each gets notification → sign independently

User Story: As a signer I see pending signature requests in my Action Required section
Acceptance Criteria: Action card links to doc → review → sign

User Story: As a user all signatures include audit trail (timestamp / IP / identity / doc hash)
Acceptance Criteria: Audit data stored per signature. Viewable in document detail

User Story: As a user when all signers complete a tier-gated signature the journey advances automatically
Acceptance Criteria: Completion triggers stage/tier transition. Both parties notified

User Story: As an admin I can create signature templates for common documents
Acceptance Criteria: Reusable templates: NDA / MOU / term sheet

User Story: As a user I see my portfolio dashboard with all assets
Acceptance Criteria: Visual summary: asset list / total value / sector breakdown

User Story: As a user I can add portfolio entries (company / project / asset / program) with structured fields
Acceptance Criteria: Add form: name / type / status / sector / valuation / description

User Story: As an investor I see a deal tracker with active investments / committed capital / pipeline / exits
Acceptance Criteria: Investor-specific portfolio view with deal lifecycle

User Story: As a company I see a project/asset registry with live projects / infrastructure / products
Acceptance Criteria: Company-specific portfolio view

User Story: As a government entity I see a program registry with bilateral agreements / MOUs / mandates
Acceptance Criteria: Government-specific portfolio view

User Story: As a user I can generate a shareable permission-controlled portfolio view
Acceptance Criteria: Generate read-only link with token. Choose which entries to include

User Story: Wider Labs generates portfolio health insights and diversification analysis
Acceptance Criteria: AI-powered gap analysis / overconcentration warnings / suggestions

User Story: As a startup/company I can build a structured financial model via guided form
Acceptance Criteria: Form: revenue model / revenue / funding ask / use of funds / runway / team / traction

User Story: As a startup/company I choose a model type (pre-revenue / revenue-stage / infra co-invest / gov program)
Acceptance Criteria: Type selection drives which form fields appear

User Story: As a startup/company my model renders as a standardised financial summary card
Acceptance Criteria: Card format: key metrics / ask / traction — comparable across models

User Story: As a startup/company my model is only visible to matched + accepted investors
Acceptance Criteria: Visibility check: must have mutual match before model data is served

User Story: As an investor I can express interest in a financial model with one action
Acceptance Criteria: Interest button → notifies company → initiates M3 relationship if not exists

User Story: As a startup/company I can update my model and interested investors are notified
Acceptance Criteria: Versioning via Mujarrad. Notification to investors who expressed interest

User Story: Wider Labs generates AI commentary on financial model (strengths / risks / comparables)
Acceptance Criteria: AI analysis card alongside financial summary

User Story: As a user in an engaged relationship I can start an integration journey (both parties opt in)
Acceptance Criteria: Launch button on relationship detail when status = 'engaged'. Both must confirm

User Story: As a user I see a journey progress tracker with tier / completion % / next action
Acceptance Criteria: Visual stepper: Tier 1 → 2 (MVP). Shows current position + next required action

User Story: Tier 1 — Service Level: define first action and both parties sign off digitally
Acceptance Criteria: Form to define action/service. Digital signature gate for both parties

User Story: Tier 2 — Business Level: upload MOU / define project scope / assign lead / set milestones
Acceptance Criteria: MOU upload + project form + milestone tracker. SIA notified for facilitation

User Story: As a user each journey tier has a document vault for auditable storage
Acceptance Criteria: Documents organized by tier within journey. Immutable once signed

User Story: As a user I receive step-by-step notifications guiding me to the next action
Acceptance Criteria: In-app + email prompts when action is needed

User Story: Tier 3 — Company Level: co-founder matching / equity / incorporation / term sheet / legal review
Acceptance Criteria: Full company formation workflow. Legal review gate

User Story: Tier 4A — Regulatory (Existing): SIA allocates regulations / compliance checklist generated
Acceptance Criteria: Country+sector-specific checklist. SIA assigns relevant frameworks

User Story: Tier 4B — Regulatory (New Frontier): flagged for SIA government relations escalation
Acceptance Criteria: Escalation request logged + tracked in admin panel

User Story: Tier 5 — Diplomatic: SIA admin approval / formal bilateral program entry
Acceptance Criteria: Admin approval gate. Gov entity portal entry created

User Story: As an admin I see a partner verification queue to approve/reject badges
Acceptance Criteria: Queue of unverified partners. View docs. Approve/reject with reason

User Story: As an admin I can manually create a match between two partners
Acceptance Criteria: Search + select two partners → create match → both notified

User Story: As an admin I see all active relationships with health and tier status
Acceptance Criteria: Table view: all relationships. Filter by status / tier / sector

User Story: As an admin I see a simplified analytics dashboard
Acceptance Criteria: Counts: active partners / matches / relationships / journeys / models

User Story: As an admin I can send platform-wide or targeted announcements
Acceptance Criteria: Compose announcement. Select audience (all / by type / specific partners)

User Story: As an admin I see an integration escalation queue for Tier 4B and 5
Acceptance Criteria: Queue of escalated partnerships requiring gov relations support

User Story: As a user I see in-app toast notifications for key events
Acceptance Criteria: Toast via sonner on: signature request / stage change / match / invitation

User Story: As a user I receive email notifications for signature requests and stage changes
Acceptance Criteria: Email sent via backend. Unsubscribe option

User Story: As a user I can configure my notification preferences
Acceptance Criteria: Per-event toggle: in-app / email / off

User Story: As a user I can switch between English and Arabic (RTL)
Acceptance Criteria: Language toggle. All UI / forms / labels switch. RTL layout applied

User Story: As a user I can use the platform in Bahasa Malaysia
Acceptance Criteria: Third language option. All strings translated

User Story: All state changes / signatures / uploads are logged immutably via Mujarrad versioning
Acceptance Criteria: Automatic via Mujarrad node versioning. No user action needed

User Story: As an admin I can export an audit report for compliance
Acceptance Criteria: Export PDF/CSV of all actions for a partner / relationship / journey

User Story: Page load under 2 seconds on all core pages
Acceptance Criteria: Lighthouse performance score > 90

User Story: Mobile-responsive across all core modules
Acceptance Criteria: All pages usable on 375px viewport

User Story: E2E encryption for all documents
Acceptance Criteria: Documents encrypted at rest and in transit

User Story: PDPA (Malaysia) and PDPL (Saudi Arabia) compliant data handling
Acceptance Criteria: Data residency. Consent management. Deletion rights

User Story: 99.5% uptime with incident response under 4 hours
Acceptance Criteria: Monitoring + alerting configured

User Story: Architecture supports 500 concurrent partners scalable to 5000
Acceptance Criteria: Load tested. No hard bottlenecks
